text:
He’s back! And Iceland is still too unreal for our favorite skeptic, because this time he’s doubling down on a ‘notorious hoax’: the northern lights 🕵️ Even with aurora expert @Sævar Helgi Bragason bringing the facts. Have you seen the aurora? ✨

#icelandair #iceland #northernlights #unreal #traveltok

text:
And that’s a wrap for 2025! ✨ As well as connecting many of you with your favorite people and places, we kept busy this year – welcoming new Airbuses, farewelling special liveries, and sponsoring events to celebrate music, design, equality, and more. We proved that Iceland is stunningly real (no AI help needed!), and we introduced the world to our weird and cheeky Yule Cat.



2026 is looking bright, with our network continuing to grow. This year we expanded to Nashville, Miami, Malaga, Edinburgh, and Istanbul, plus a new domestic gateway in Höfn. Next year we’ll reach Venice and Faro – we can’t wait! We’ll even experience a total solar eclipse in Iceland in August 🎉



We hope 2026 holds lots of joyful adventures for you, too. From all of us, Happy New Year! ✈️

#iceland #icelandair #NYE #reykjavik

text:
The streets of Reykjavík have fallen almost silent tonight – something big is happening! Iceland’s handball team is stepping onto the court to face Denmark in the semi-final of the European Men’s Handball Championship, and it feels like the whole nation is glued to a screen, cheering them on. We couldn’t be prouder of the team. Áfram Ísland! 🇮🇸 

#icelandair #iceland #handball

authorMeta.name: icelandair_official
authorMeta.signature: We bring the spirit of Iceland to the world ✈️

text:
The tale of Iceland’s Yule Cat might surprise you. It tells of a giant cat that prowls the winter nights, devouring those who don’t get new clothes for Christmas. Not exactly festive, right? Yet underneath lies a beautiful reminder to care for one another and to share warmth when it’s needed most ✨ And that’s why the story of the Yule Cat, however un-Christmassy it seems, deserves to take flight.  

We bring the Yule spirit of Iceland to the world ✈️

#icelandair #iceland #yulecat #icelandicfolklore #storytime

text:
2025 was a record-breaking year for Icelandair, busy with new destinations, new aircraft, an attention-seeking Yule Cat, and much more. We flew 5.1 million passengers – our highest number ever – to over 60 destinations ✈️ Check out some of our highlights! We look forward to more adventures with all of you in 2026 💙

#icelandair #iceland #traveltok #2025

text:
What does Iceland look like to you? Your answer might be influenced by photos you’ve seen of our incredible country ❤️ We spoke to five artists based in Iceland about how they view photography, creativity, and of course Iceland itself. Whether you’re a photographer or not, their perspectives might come in handy next time you visit us 😍

#iceland #icelandair #photograhy #travel
